description_long: This object consists of a set of modular concrete jersey barriers arranged in a segmented line, each topped with stacked wooden pallets and some metal rods or pipes. The barriers are robust and rectangular, with a solid concrete base and a slightly rough, weathered surface. The wooden pallets are stacked in varying heights, adding a makeshift, utilitarian appearance, and are secured with metal railings or frames. The central section features additional vertical metal pipes, possibly for reinforcement or added security. The concrete shows signs of exposure to the elements, with some discoloration and minor surface wear. This configuration is typical for temporary access control or construction site protection in urban environments.
description: A segmented row of concrete jersey barriers topped with wooden pallets and metal railings, showing weathered surfaces and utilitarian construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full arrangement of concrete barriers, wooden pallets, and metal railings, with the central section featuring vertical metal pipes.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the barriers and the stacked pallets, emphasizing the depth and separation between segments.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the barriers and pallets, with the central section's pipes and a circular opening visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the linear arrangement and the layered construction of the barriers and pallets.
